headline: GOLD HOLDS $4,600 AS SILVER SURGES TO $74+ — JAPAN FX MOVE CRUSHES THE DOLLAR
snippet: Gold steadied near $4,600/oz Friday while silver extended gains above $74, boosted by a sharp USD depreciation after Japan intervened in currency markets. Gold is up 42% year-over-year; silver has more than doubled in 12 months, with AI capex and solar manufacturing demand underpinning industrial silver consumption.

headline: AGNICO EAGLE SHATTERS RECORDS: $1.71B Q1 NET INCOME, $730M FREE CASH FLOW AT $4,861 GOLD
snippet: Agnico Eagle reported its strongest-ever quarterly adjusted net income of $1.71B ($3.41/share) on record operating margins, driven by a realized gold price of $4,861/oz. The company generated $730M in free cash flow and returned $375M to shareholders via dividends and buybacks in Q1 2026.

headline: ABRASILVER HITS 68.5 METRES OF 343 G/T SILVER AT DIABLILLOS — GRADE CONTINUITY CONFIRMED
snippet: AbraSilver Resource's Phase VI drill program at Diablillos, Argentina confirmed 68.5m grading 343 g/t silver in hole DDH 26-012, including a 17m interval at 917 g/t. Results extend the JAC-Oculto trend and support an impending resource update at what is emerging as a significant silver deposit in the Puna region.

headline: OUTCROP SILVER CONFIRMS HIGH-GRADE SHOOTS AT GUADUAL — RESOURCE UPDATE DUE THIS QUARTER
snippet: Outcrop Silver & Gold's Santa Ana project in Colombia confirmed high-grade silver shoot continuity at Guadual, with best intercepts up to 2,844 g/t AgEq over 0.84m estimated true width. A three-rig program is advancing and an updated mineral resource estimate is expected this quarter.

headline: MUSTANG ENERGY LOGS 4.9M AT 2.69% COPPER AT SURPRISE CREEK URANIUM-COPPER, SASK.
snippet: Mustang Energy released first drill assays from its Surprise Creek uranium-copper project northwest of the Athabasca Basin: best hole returned 4.9m at 2.69% copper including 0.5m at 15.9% copper. Earlier surface grabs had returned uranium grades up to 9.39% U3O8, making this a multi-element poly-metallic play worth tracking.

headline: MINING M&A HITS $21.6B IN Q1 2026 — STRONGEST QUARTER IN 3 YEARS AS GOLD DRIVES DEALS
snippet: Global mining M&A reached $21.6B across 121 transactions in Q1 2026, the strongest start to a year since 2023. Headline deals include Agnico Eagle's $3.8B three-deal Finland consolidation and USA Rare Earth's $2.8B acquisition of Serra Verde Group. High gold prices and critical mineral demand are driving the pace of consolidation.

headline: HAYWOOD: GOLD EQUITIES STILL CHEAP AT $4,600 GOLD — 'STRUCTURAL BULL' JUST GETTING STARTED
snippet: Haywood Securities analysts say senior gold producers are trading at just 9.6x forward cash flow — barely above the five-year average — despite gold near $4,600/oz. The firm forecasts gold averaging $4,906/oz in 2026 and argues mining equities have yet to fully re-rate to reflect stronger underlying metal prices. Recent pullbacks are called an attractive entry point.

headline: SILVER AND GOLD BOOM TURNS COPPER PRODUCTION COSTS NEGATIVE FOR SOUTHERN, VALE
snippet: Surging byproduct credits from silver (up 150%+ year-over-year) and gold (up 42%) have pushed the effective copper production costs at Southern Copper Corp. and Vale into negative territory. The data point illustrates how the precious metals repricing is reshaping economics across the entire mining complex — not just for gold producers.

headline: COT SILVER REPORT: RECORD CHINA IMPORTS AND AI CAPEX UNDERPIN $74+ SILVER
snippet: The weekly Commitments of Traders silver report provides fresh positioning data as silver trades above $74/oz — more than double a year ago. Context: China imported a record 836 tons in March 2026 (2.7x the 10-year seasonal average) on solar manufacturing front-loading and retail demand. AI capex of $715B from Meta, Alphabet, Microsoft and Amazon further underpins industrial silver demand.

headline: GOLD, SILVER, COPPER RALLY COMING: WHY BARRICK AND NEWMONT LOOK CHEAP RIGHT NOW
snippet: An independent analyst argues that Barrick Gold (NYSE: B), Newmont (NYSE: NEM), and junior Power Metallic Mines offer the best risk-reward in the sector as a multi-commodity rally takes shape across gold, platinum, silver, and copper. The core thesis: mining equities lag behind metal prices and the gap is about to close.

headline: RARE EARTH MOMENTUM: CHINA TIGHTENS SUPPLY, U.S. 'PROJECT VAULT' RAMPS UP
snippet: Rare earth equities gained momentum in late April as China Northern Rare Earth Group signalled Q2 2026 price hikes, domestic inventories sit at historic lows, and the Trump administration's $12B 'Project Vault' critical mineral stockpile takes shape. USA Rare Earth's $2.8B Serra Verde acquisition underscores the urgency to build non-China rare earth supply chains.

headline: F3 URANIUM EXTENDS TETRA ZONE STEP-OUTS AT PATTERSON LAKE NORTH, ATHABASCA BASIN
snippet: F3 Uranium's winter 2026 step-out drilling at the Tetra Zone, Patterson Lake North confirmed uranium mineralization 275m along strike from the high-grade JR Zone. Previous best hole: PLN25-219A returned 13.0m of 0.28% U3O8 including 3.0m at 1.19%. F3 is fully funded for aggressive 2026 expansion drilling across the broader Broach target system.
